name: Ermita de Butsénit, Rio Segre and Rufea wetlands — Lérida
name_original: Ermita de Butsénit, Río Segre y humedales de Rufea — Lérida
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails around the Segre region are characterized by diverse landscapes, including river valleys, limestone cliffs, and the foothills of the central Pyrenees. The area is shaped by the Segre River, offering routes that wind through natural areas and past historical sites. Hikers can explore a mix of Mediterranean and continental natural elements, from plains and fruit tree plantations to forested mountains. This varied topography provides a range of options for family outings. Best family friendly hiking trails around the Segre The most popular family friendly hiking route is Passet and Font-Vives Lakes Loop, a 5.9 miles (9.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete, offering views of the lakes and surrounding natural environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Pala Alta and Montalegre Hermitage Loop, a moderate 8.8 miles (14.2 km) path that explores the higher terrain and includes a historical hermitage. Local hikers also love the La Seu d'Urgell and Calbinyà Loop Trail, a 5.8 miles (9.3 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and rural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 56 minutes. Hiking around the Segre is defined by river valleys, Pyrenean foothills, and protected natural areas, offering a network of routes suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Segre are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 2700 reviews. More than 26500 hikers have used komoot to explore Segre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in the Segre region?
A: The Segre region offers a wide variety of options for families, with over 240 hiking trails suitable for different ages and abilities. You'll find plenty of routes to explore with your loved ones.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in the Segre region?
A: The Segre region boasts incredibly diverse landscapes, perfect for family adventures. You can expect everything from gentle riverside paths and trails through fruit tree plantations to routes winding through valleys, hills, and the foothills of the Pyrenees. Many trails feature impressive limestone cliffs, reservoirs, and protected natural areas.

Q: When is the best time of year to go hiking with family in the Segre region?
A: The Segre region is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer can be great for trails near water features like reservoirs or in higher, shadier areas. Always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there easy hiking options suitable for young children or beginners in Segre?
A: Absolutely! There are over 80 easy hiking routes in the Segre region, perfect for families with young children or those new to hiking. These trails often feature minimal elevation gain and shorter distances, making them accessible and enjoyable for everyone.

Q: Can we find hikes along the Segre River or near reservoirs?
A: Yes, the Segre River is a central feature of the region, and many family-friendly trails follow its course or lead to its numerous reservoirs. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e919466122/segre-river-nature-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segre River Nature Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a pleasant experience along the water, and areas like the Aiguabarreig Segre-Noguera Pallaresa Protected Natural Area are rich with water features and wildlife.

Q: Are there any historical or natural landmarks to explore on family hikes?
A: The Segre region is rich in both natural and historical attractions. You can discover fascinating sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4972766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Seu Vella de Lleida&lt;/a&gt;, an old cathedral offering panoramic views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5187758" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tòfol Footbridge&lt;/a&gt; which connects natural paths. Natural wonders include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4131150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova del Tabac&lt;/a&gt; cave and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5581211" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parc de la Mitjana&lt;/a&gt; forest. Many trails pass by or lead to these points of interest.

Q: Are dogs allowed on family-friendly hiking trails in the Segre region?
A: Many trails in the Segre region are dog-friendly, but it's always best to check specific route details or local regulations, especially within protected natural areas. Generally, keeping your dog on a leash and cleaning up after them is expected.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family-friendly trails in the Segre region?
A: The Segre region is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.56 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the tranquility of the river valleys, and the opportunity to combine nature walks with visits to historical sites. The well-maintained trails and the variety of options for all skill levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Can you recommend a family-friendly hike that includes lakes?
A: For a lovely family hike featuring lakes, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e958890186/passet-and-font-vives-lakes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passet and Font-Vives Lakes Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route offers beautiful scenery around the water, providing a refreshing experience for the whole family.

Q: Are there any family hikes that offer views of historical sites or cultural landmarks?
A: Yes, several routes combine natural beauty with cultural exploration. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e934004407/pala-alta-and-montalegre-hermitage-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pala Alta and Montalegre Hermitage Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading you through scenic landscapes while offering glimpses of historical structures like the Montalegre Hermitage.

Q: Are there any circular routes suitable for families in the Segre region?
A: Many family-friendly trails in the Segre region are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point without retracing your steps. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937136929/la-seu-d-urgell-and-calbinya-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Seu d'Urgell and Calbinyà Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate option that offers varied scenery in a convenient loop format.

Q: Is parking generally available at the trailheads for family hikes?
A: In many areas of the Segre region, especially near popular trailheads and attractions, you will find designated parking facilities. However, it's always a good idea to check specific route information or local maps for parking availability before you set off, particularly during peak seasons.
tips: The Church is surrounded by rural houses and fruit fields. The story goes that an ox ran into an object, which its shepherd recognized as an image of Mary. He took it with devotion and presented it to his neighbors, who decided to dedicate a place of worship to worship it.
Lever over the Segre River that belongs to the town of Montoliu de Lérida.
Long concrete walkway that will help us cross the Segre River (near the water) through the Rufea area.
From this point we will be able to observe the different birds found in the Rufea wetland. It is recommended to go with binoculars.
